title: Persuasive Games
creator: Ian Bogost
description: An argument that computational rules and processes can make claims about how systems work. Case studies examine games related to politics, advertising, and education and develop the idea of procedural rhetoric.
useful: A foundational, contestable framework for analyzing meaning produced by rules rather than only by story or imagery.
audience: Advanced students, critics, and designers studying how systems make arguments.
learning: Define procedural rhetoric in relation to game rules
Analyze how a simulation frames a real-world system
Critique the limits of persuasive-design claims
